description: Daisy and James, in a relaxed moment at the mining town, bond over drinks and pinball. James teases Daisy about her feelings and connections with S.H.I.E.L.D. and Lincoln. Their conversation reveals James’ loneliness and philosophy about sides and loyalty, setting up his later role in Hive's plans. The lighter banter is tinged with underlying wariness and tension.

transcript.line: One Martian Multi-ball, three Bumper Blasters, and a 3-Way Rocket Combo. Boom! Good luck beating that score.
Better keep your hat on, Dundee, ‘cause somebody don't play when it comes to pinball.
Mmm. Ah! This beer is bloody wonderful.
I thought Aussies hated American beer.
Only when we got to pay for it. It is good to be back in the real world again.
Yeah, what made you go all Ted Kaczynski in that trailer, anyway?
Don't know, really. Just never found people to be my cup of tea. They didn't like me. I didn't like them, so I thought I'd do everyone a favor and move as far away as humanly possible.
That sounds pretty lonely.
Yeah. Never really figured on that part.
Aww. You're a little baby bird, all fixed up and back in the wild.
I'm not keen on infantilizing, but I would be willing to let it slide for a proper dance.
Oh. Okay.
Whoo!
Okay. Let's don't get your rhinestones in a bunch, cowboy. Let's just keep it to pinball.
Please tell me you're not pining over Stinkin' Lincoln.
You know, James, just because you don't care about anyone else doesn't mean the rest of us don't.
Sorry. I didn't mean to offend. It's just... you were S.H.I.E.L.D. So those people you care about are the same ones that are trying to stop what we're doing here.
It's not their fault. They don't know any better.
Doesn't matter if they know any better or not. They're on the opposite side. I can't see any way it doesn't end badly for them.
There might be one way.
Oh, yeah? And what's that?
We make them understand.
